canonical_endpoint.per_order_formula: IF(risk_active, IF(APj > 0, APj / LTj, 0.5 * (1 / RPj)), 1 - ((Bt_at_request + Ut_at_request) / j))
canonical_endpoint.aggregation: Unclipped mean over completed non-lost workbook-visible rows emitted in original j/OPTj order; original j is preserved and lost or horizon-unresolved orders emit no primary row.

request_snapshot_semantics.capture_time: At OPTj, before inserting request j into the Op9 queue.
request_snapshot_semantics.immutability: Bt/Ut are frozen on the order record at request generation and are never reconstructed from OATj or rewritten by later release, completion or loss events.
request_snapshot_semantics.same_timestamp_convention: Events already scheduled before the request-generation callback run first; then Bt/Ut are snapshotted; then request j is enqueued. The event sequence is recorded.

mandatory_guardrails: remaining backlog and maximum backlog age
canonical_endpoint.decision: This is the source-aligned canonical development and future-confirmation contract. Its implementation does not restore any prior result. No Paper-2 null, positive, H_PI, H_obs or ceiling may be asserted until the same tapes and complete same-contract comparator are rescored under v2 and all guardrails are rebuilt.

excel_formula_reaudit.scope: Exact formula replay given workbook Bt/Ut inputs only.

v2_implementation_audit.claim_limit: Workbook replay with injected source Bt/Ut snapshots does not prove endogenous DES ledger fidelity, adaptive headroom or a Paper-2 result.

ret_excel_visible_v1.implementation_defect: The OAT-derived Bt/Ut reconstruction is not source-validated. The workbook rows are ordered by j/OPTj and carry request-time ledger evidence; treating Bt/Ut as OAT-time cumulative observations changes the estimand.
ret_excel_visible_v1.allowed_claim: Only the narrow historical statement that the unchanged cell formula exactly reproduces cached workbook cells when workbook Bt/Ut are supplied as inputs.
prohibited_claims: terminal Return A or Return B evidence
ret_excel_visible_v1.restoration_rule: No old scalar can be relabelled. Re-run or rescore the identical eligible tapes under v2, rebuild every same-contract comparator and guardrail, then issue a new versioned verdict.

quarantine_registry.scope: Every result whose scored primary ledger was ret_excel_visible_v1 or an equivalent OAT-derived Bt/Ut implementation, including historical D-K3 screens where applicable.
Program H visible-v1 repairs, observable screens, bounds and comparator replays.
Program J visible-v1 complete-frontier and observable-policy repairs.
All M/T/R <=3, <=4, W12/W16/W24 local or VPS outputs scored with visible-v1, including watched or deep-replayed outputs.

legacy_nonmonotonicity_reclassification.old_observation: The OAT-derived sparse visible-v1 ledger can rank a later-batched trajectory above an earlier-completing trajectory.
legacy_nonmonotonicity_reclassification.current_interpretation: This is evidence against the superseded OAT-ledger implementation, not an intrinsic property of ret_excel_request_snapshot_v2 and not a canonical K3 ceiling.

garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.scope: Factory-level aggregate production planning in an MTS facility over 36 weeks; demand variability is the sole uncertainty; seven fixed pure APP substrategies are compared.
garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.calibration: Coefficients are calibrated to the paper's hypothetical factory and seven-strategy ensemble, not to the MFSC distribution system.
garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.dominant_static_warning: The paper reports S12 as the dominant fixed substrategy; it does not establish sequential state feedback or learning value.

metric_decision.current_paper2: Cobb-Douglas may be reported only as a frozen secondary construct-sensitivity outcome. It cannot rescue any visible-v1 output or substitute for a future v2 result.
metric_decision.separate_factory_resilience_lane: Possible only as a separately preregistered factory/APP study with validated APP ledgers, justified calibration and complete same-contract comparators.
metric_decision.other_metrics: No endpoint may be selected after observing policy rankings. A new primary construct requires domain authority, a frozen estimand and a separate claim.

risk_governance.allowed: Thesis risks may be edited within author-supported ranges, and a realistic new risk may be introduced as a disclosed researcher extension.
required_before_screen: source or exact Garrido sign-off question
plausible envelope frozen independently of policy results
risk_governance.forbidden: Increasing frequency or impact until a learner or preferred metric wins, or selecting a metric because it creates headroom.
